title: Platform Adapters: Aider, Gemini CLI, Codex CLI
description: Build and validate SpawnAdapter implementations for three additional platforms beyond Claude Code: Aider, Gemini CLI, Codex CLI. Minimal viable adapters — spawn-and-parse, not full feature parity. Document gaps per platform.

dod.criterion: AiderSpawnAdapter and GeminiCLISpawnAdapter implement SpawnAdapter interface and type-check
CodexCLISpawnAdapter verified against current Codex CLI docs with bugs fixed
PLATFORM-ADAPTERS.md documents capability gaps across all 4 adapters
getSpawnAdapter(platform) factory returns correct adapter by platform name

tasks.title: Research Aider CLI interface and build AiderSpawnAdapter
tasks.user_scenario: When the conductor is configured for Aider, it can spawn an Aider agent process and track its completion.
tasks.scope: Research Aider CLI non-interactive mode. Build AiderSpawnAdapter implementing SpawnAdapter interface. Register export.

dod.criterion: server/platform/aider-spawn.ts exists, exports AiderSpawnAdapter implementing SpawnAdapter interface
buildArgs maps SpawnConfig fields to correct Aider CLI flags with comments explaining unsupported fields
Both spawnDetached and spawnTracked modes work with correct binary name and argument structure
AiderSpawnAdapter is exported from server/platform/index.ts
npm run type-check passes with zero errors

tasks.title: Research Gemini CLI interface and build GeminiCLISpawnAdapter
tasks.user_scenario: When the conductor is configured for Gemini CLI, it can spawn a Gemini agent process and track its completion.
tasks.scope: Research Gemini CLI scripted mode. Build GeminiCLISpawnAdapter implementing SpawnAdapter interface. Register export.

dod.criterion: server/platform/gemini-cli-spawn.ts exists, exports GeminiCLISpawnAdapter implementing SpawnAdapter interface
buildArgs maps SpawnConfig fields to correct Gemini CLI flags with comments explaining unsupported fields
Both spawnDetached and spawnTracked modes work with correct binary name and argument structure
GeminiCLISpawnAdapter is exported from server/platform/index.ts
npm run type-check passes with zero errors

tasks.title: Verify and fix CodexCLISpawnAdapter (zero-trust audit)
tasks.user_scenario: The existing Codex CLI adapter actually works correctly when Codex CLI is available.
tasks.scope: Zero-trust review of codex-cli-spawn.ts. Verify imports, flags, and interface. Fix issues found.

dod.criterion: personality-compiler import verified: compilePersonality signature matches usage, or adapter updated
Codex CLI flags verified against current docs: -q flag confirmed or corrected
Any bugs or outdated assumptions documented as code comments with TODO markers
npm run type-check passes with zero errors

tasks.title: Document platform capability gaps and adapter registry
tasks.user_scenario: A developer evaluating platforms can read a single document showing what each adapter supports and how to add new adapters.
tasks.scope: Create PLATFORM-ADAPTERS.md with comparison table. Create getSpawnAdapter factory function in server/platform/index.ts.

dod.criterion: server/platform/PLATFORM-ADAPTERS.md exists with comparison table across all 4 adapters
Each adapter's known limitations documented
A getSpawnAdapter(platform) factory exists in server/platform/index.ts
npm run type-check passes with zero errors

brainstorm_alignment.followed: Implemented all adapters following the exact same structural pattern as ClaudeCodeSpawnAdapter (buildArgs/buildEnv/spawnDetached/spawnTracked). Mapped SpawnConfig fields to each platform's CLI flags with explicit comments for unsupported fields. Created factory with exhaustive switch and SpawnPlatform type union.
brainstorm_alignment.deviated: None -- all tasks executed as specified.

proposed_improvements: Extract common spawn boilerplate (spawnDetached/spawnTracked/killAgent/buildEnv) into a BaseSpawnAdapter abstract class -- all 4 adapters share identical implementations for these methods, only buildArgs and pre-spawn hooks differ.
Add runtime validation that the target binary exists on PATH before spawning (e.g. `which aider`) to give a clear error instead of a cryptic ENOENT from child_process.
The Codex adapter's codex.md write-before-spawn pattern could race when multiple agents target the same cwd. Consider writing to a temp file or using a lock.
Consider adding a SpawnConfig.timeout field so tracked spawns can auto-kill after a deadline -- useful for pipeline steps that should not run indefinitely.
Gemini CLI's flag interface is new and evolving. Pin a known-good version in documentation and re-verify flags periodically.
user_walkthrough: The getSpawnAdapter('aider') factory call returns an AiderSpawnAdapter. Calling spawnAgent({ prompt: 'fix the bug', cwd: '/repo' }, 'tracked') spawns `aider --yes-always --no-git --message 'fix the bug'` and returns a handle with a promise that resolves when Aider exits. The same pattern works for gemini-cli (spawns `gemini -p 'fix the bug'`) and codex-cli (spawns `codex -q 'fix the bug'`). Each adapter documents which SpawnConfig fields are supported vs silently ignored. PLATFORM-ADAPTERS.md provides a single comparison table.
